name: Linda's Potato Skins
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrub the potatoes clean and dry them thoroughly.
2. Pierce each potato several times with a fork.
3. Arrange the potatoes on a baking sheet and bake in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for about 1 hour, or until tender. Let cool enough to handle before cutting.
4. Cut each potato in half lengthwise and scoop out the center, leaving about an 1/8 inch (0.25 cm) of potato in the shell. Cover the shells.
5. Chill the prepared shells for up to 2 days before using them for the skins.
6. If desired, add 1/3 cup milk, 1/4 cup butter, salt, and pepper to the centers of the potatoes for mashed potatoes.
7. Preheat the oven to 500°F (260°C).
8. Melt the 1/3 cup butter in a separate bowl.
9. Brush each shell with the melted butter on both sides.
10. Place the cut side up on the baking sheet and bake for 10 minutes, or until brown and crisp.
11. Distribute some bacon, onion, and cheese into each shell.
12. Bake just until the cheese melts.
13. Serve hot. If the potatoes are large, you may want to cut them in half crosswise before serving.
